status: Partially fixed in source change pending release
finding: batch checkpoint can partially mutate and close the queue-head task before failing on the next queued task's owner mismatch. This violates the playbook expectation that checkpoint is the safe boundary before commit.
expectedBehavior: batch checkpoint should be atomic per queue head: either finish the current queue-head checkpoint and return a committable bundle, or fail before mutating close state. It should not close the queue head and then fail because the next queued task is blocked/owned by another actor.
evidenceOrRepro: On 2026-07-19, after runner-sync build/release for ATM-FRAMEWORK-TEMP-codex-plan-series-skill, `node atm.mjs batch checkpoint --actor codex-governance-optimizer --json` failed with ATM_TASK_CLOSE_OWNER_MISMATCH for TASK-SYNC-0001 owned by codex-atm-gov-0156. Immediately afterward, `node atm.mjs tasks status --task ATM-FRAMEWORK-TEMP-codex-plan-series-skill --json` showed the queue head live ledger as done/released with close and lock-cleanup events, leaving closure packet, runner-sync receipt, release artifacts, and task events staged/dirty even though checkpoint returned failure.
followUp: Immediate ghost-task cause fixed in packages/cli/src/commands/task-direction.ts by filtering terminal ledger tasks when creating queues, skipping terminal ledger tasks when advancing legacy queues, and completing terminal-only queues. Batch runtime also normalizes terminal-only active batches in packages/cli/src/commands/work-channels.ts. Imported task discovery now excludes terminal ledger tasks in packages/cli/src/commands/next/route-resolution/queue-inspection.ts, so explicitly mentioning TASK-SYNC-0001 no longer resurrects the superseded task. Regression coverage lives in task-direction-queue.test.ts; 2026-07-19 source-first verification returned no active batch run after TASK-SYNC-0001 normalization and then openTaskCount:0/task-scope-not-found for a prompt mentioning TASK-SYNC-0001. Remaining follow-up: add regression coverage for batch checkpoint with a closeable queue head followed by a genuinely blocked/foreign-owned next task, and fix checkpoint to avoid next-task owner checks until after the current queue-head commit boundary, or make next-task claiming advisory after a successful current checkpoint result.
